event_analyses.title: CORE-USDT-SWAP price crash -48.7% with OI deviation 58.34%

causal_chain.name: Low market cap token with thin liquidity
causal_chain.description: CORE is a low market cap token with thin order book depth (avg ~$51,408). The shallow liquidity makes it highly susceptible to price manipulation and cascading liquidations from relatively small order sizes.
causal_chain.detail: Order book depth averaged approximately $51,408 across bid/ask sides. Total OI USD only $2.54M. Low liquidity amplifies any directional pressure and enables outsized price moves from moderate capital deployment.

causal_chain.name: Massive user influx — +121% in 33 hours
causal_chain.description: Total users holding CORE-USDT-SWAP positions surged from 7,215 (2026-03-29 00:00) to 15,972 (2026-03-30 08:00), a 121% increase. The surge accelerated dramatically after 2026-03-29 16:00, with user count nearly doubling from 7,341 to 15,972 in just 16 hours.
causal_chain.detail: Hourly position data shows the influx began slowly (7,215 to 7,341 over 16 hours) then exploded: 7,341 at 16:00 → 8,136 at 17:00 → 9,230 at 18:00 → 10,193 at 19:00 → 11,923 at 20:00 → 13,019 at 21:00. Short positions consistently outnumber longs at ~1.85:1 ratio throughout the surge.

risk_assessment.detail: 121% user surge in 33 hours is highly abnormal for a low-cap token. The consistent ~78% short dominance throughout the influx suggests coordinated directional betting or herding behavior ahead of or during the crash.

causal_chain.name: Price crash -48.7%, OI deviation 58.34% triggered platform alarm
causal_chain.description: CORE price crashed from 0.06544 to 0.03359 (-48.7%) within 24 hours, hitting a low of 0.0291 (-55.5%). Open interest deviation reached 58.34%, well above the 20% threshold, triggering the platform's risk monitoring alarm.
causal_chain.detail: Market data: last=0.03359, open24h=0.06544, high24h=0.06621, low24h=0.0291. OI=75,770,300 contracts ($2,543,608.97 USD). Funding rate deeply negative at -0.0001479 indicating extreme short pressure. 24h volume=2,451,427,713 contracts — massive volume relative to OI.

risk_assessment.detail: 58.34% OI deviation far exceeds 20% threshold. -48.7% price move in 24h is extreme. Negative funding rate confirms overwhelming short pressure. Volume/OI ratio suggests rapid turnover and potential cascading liquidations.

causal_chain.name: Short-heavy concentration and depleted-equity positions
causal_chain.description: As of 2026-03-30 08:00, short positions (12,487) outnumber longs (6,754) at a 1.85:1 ratio. Multiple flagged users hold positions with near-zero equity — including accounts with $0.037 and $0.17 equity holding leveraged CORE positions through the crash.
causal_chain.detail: Short/long ratio 1.85:1 (12,487 vs 6,754). Flagged users: master_user_id=8425308 has $0.037 equity on a CORE short; master_user_id=3438667 has $0.17 equity on a CORE long (isolated margin — likely liquidated). master_user_id=2044005 has $65.51 equity but $475M historical volume. These near-zero-equity accounts amplify liquidation cascade risk.

risk_assessment.detail: Depleted-equity positions on both sides create liquidation cascade risk. Short-heavy imbalance means any short squeeze could trigger mass liquidations. Conversely, continued downward pressure liquidates the minority longs, accelerating the crash.

event_analyses.title: SOPH-USDT-SWAP price down restricted with OI deviation 55.04%

causal_chain.name: Low liquidity token with shallow depth
causal_chain.description: SOPH-USDT-SWAP has very low order book depth (~$21,207) making it highly vulnerable to price manipulation. OI USD is only $716,159 — a single moderately-sized position can represent a significant portion of total open interest.
causal_chain.detail: Total OI USD $716,159.10 with depth approximately $21,207. The low liquidity means even modest selling pressure can trigger price restriction mechanisms. Token is susceptible to outsized price moves.

causal_chain.name: Sudden user surge +16.4% in 4-hour window
causal_chain.description: Total SOPH users holding positions increased from ~6,688 baseline to 7,788 (+16.4%), with the surge concentrated between 2026-03-30 04:00 and 2026-03-30 08:00. The user count had been stable around 6,700 for the prior 24 hours before the sudden spike.
causal_chain.detail: Hourly data shows stability from 2026-03-29 00:00 (6,705) through 2026-03-30 00:00 (6,679), then sharp increase: 7,292 at 04:00 → 7,461 at 05:00 → 7,586 at 06:00 → 7,788 at 08:00. Short positions drove the surge (4,982 at 00:00 → 5,745 at 08:00, +763 shorts vs +509 longs).

risk_assessment.detail: 16.4% user surge in 4 hours is abnormal for a low-liquidity token. Concentrated timing suggests potential coordinated activity or social media-driven herding.

causal_chain.name: Price down restricted (Y cap), OI deviation 55.04%
causal_chain.description: SOPH-USDT-SWAP price was restricted downward (Y cap mechanism triggered). OI deviation reached 55.04%, nearly 3x the 20% alert threshold. The 24h price range showed significant volatility (high 0.009633, low 0.007956).
causal_chain.detail: Market data: last=0.008329, open24h=0.008042, high24h=0.009633, low24h=0.007956. Change +3.6% at time of snapshot but price restriction indicates prior extreme downward move that triggered the cap. OI=85,911,600 (859,116 contracts), OI USD=$716,159.10. Funding rate=0.0000125 (near neutral).

risk_assessment.detail: 55.04% OI deviation is 2.75x the 20% threshold. Price restriction mechanism activated. Despite current +3.6% at snapshot, the Y cap indicates prior extreme downward pressure that was mechanically halted.

causal_chain.name: Short-heavy positions with depleted-equity users
causal_chain.description: Short positions dominate at 1.75:1 ratio (5,745 shorts vs 3,292 longs). Multiple flagged users hold leveraged short positions with near-zero equity, amplifying liquidation cascade risk if the price restriction lifts and a short squeeze occurs.
causal_chain.detail: Flagged users: master_user_id=3573410 has $0.057 equity on 2x SOPH short (region mismatch); master_user_id=5002143 has $10.84 equity on 2x SOPH short ($187.4M volume). master_user_id=10543257 has $81,685 equity — large enough to represent ~11.4% of total SOPH OI USD. Depleted accounts risk cascading liquidations; large accounts risk market manipulation.

risk_assessment.detail: Combination of depleted-equity short holders and one large-capital short holder creates dual risk: cascading liquidations from small accounts and potential market influence from the large account.

rule_blocks.title: Buffer Too Tight
rule_blocks.description: Flags instruments where the EMA-derived limit price is already breaching or very close to the Y cap boundary, indicating the buffer is too tight for current market conditions.

rule_blocks.title: Asymmetric Basis
rule_blocks.description: Flags perp instruments where the EMA basis is large relative to the Z cap, suggesting the cap should be widened on one side to accommodate the persistent premium/discount.

rule_blocks.title: Asset-Type Consistency
rule_blocks.description: Checks whether each instrument's Y/Z caps are consistent with the standard ranges for its asset type classification.

rule_blocks.title: Z Cap <= Y Cap
rule_blocks.description: The Z (index) cap must always be strictly greater than the Y (limit) cap. If Z <= Y the price limit hierarchy is broken.
